subject: Math
domain: Number and Operations in Base Ten

lesson.story_mission: Help the Base-Ten Builders organize blocks into hundreds flats, tens rods, and ones cubes before the number parade begins.
lesson.mini_lesson: Three-digit numbers use hundreds, tens, and ones. In 342, the 3 means 3 hundreds or 300, the 4 means 4 tens or 40, and the 2 means 2 ones. Expanded form writes the digit values as 300 + 40 + 2. Ten tens make 1 hundred.
lesson.objective: Read, build, and write three-digit numbers using hundreds, tens, and ones.
lesson.concept_explanation: A digit has a value based on its place. Hundreds are worth 100 each, tens are worth 10 each, and ones are worth 1 each.
lesson.common_misconception: A child may read the digit 4 in 342 as just 4 instead of 40 because they forget the tens place value.
lesson.real_world_connection: Place value helps us count big collections, pack groups efficiently, and read numbers in maps, scores, and money amounts.
lesson.lesson_summary: Use the chart or blocks to name hundreds, tens, and ones, then add their values for expanded form.

worked_examples.text: 342 has 3 hundreds, 4 tens, and 2 ones, so 342 = 300 + 40 + 2.
508 has 5 hundreds, 0 tens, and 8 ones, so the zero holds the tens place: 508 = 500 + 0 + 8.
10 tens can trade for 1 hundred because 10 groups of 10 make 100.

hint_ladder: Find the hundreds, tens, and ones columns first.
Say the value of each digit: hundreds are hundreds, tens are groups of ten, ones are singles.
Add the values to check your standard or expanded form.

guided_practice.prompt: Which label matches 3 hundreds, 4 tens, and 2 ones?
hints: Look at the place-value labels.
Read hundreds first, then tens, then ones.
Check by adding the values of the digits.

feedback.incorrect: Try matching each digit to its place-value column.

misconception_bank.hundreds_place_confusion: Confuses the hundreds digit with tens or ones when reading three-digit numbers.
misconception_bank.digit_value_confusion: Names the digit instead of its value in the number.
misconception_bank.expanded_form_error: Leaves out a place value or writes digits instead of values in expanded form.
misconception_bank.zero_placeholder_confusion: Ignores a zero that holds a place in a three-digit number.
misconception_bank.ten_tens_confusion: Does not yet see 100 as ten groups of ten.

review_bank.prompt: Build 342 with blocks and say each place value.
Write 508 as 500 + 0 + 8 and explain the zero.
Trade 10 tens rods for 1 hundred flat.

game_theme.title: Hundred Harbor Builders
game_theme.mission_intro: Sort hundreds flats, tens rods, and ones cubes so every parade number is built correctly.
